Now what I recommend for you guys is in your retargeting sequence you know a lot of marketers tend to just put 180 days or 30 days which is the default and if you think about it 30 days to be showing someone the same piece of creative over 30 days the same message is a little bit cumbersome you know it gets a little bit you're gonna get some ad fatigue going on there. The reality is if they if they haven't taken action after 14 days they probably need to see a different reminder or a different call to action or a different message. So what I like to do is I like to divide my retargeting audiences by say even four days 14 days and then 30 days or you could do three days six days ten days if it's a really short purchase cycle item and you can even do it longer if it's a really long purchase cycle for example if you're dealing in the automate mode of industry it takes people a long time before they're gonna actually purchase a car right. So what I like to do though is just segment my audience my retargeting audiences and you do that by controlling the number of days and then that essentially allows you to create an evergreen campaign meaning if someone visits your website within the first let's say we go 10 days here within the
